 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","title":"Define artifact manifest schema, retention, and redaction policy for verification outputs","description":"## Objective
Define a shared verification artifact manifest and retention/redaction policy for reproducible audits.

## Scope
1. Artifact manifest schema (scenario metadata, command set, checksums, outputs, logs, environment fingerprint).
2. Retention and pruning rules for CI/nightly/local runs.
3. Redaction policy for sensitive data while preserving debug value.
4. Validation tooling for manifest schema conformance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All gate outputs can be represented in the manifest schema.
- Manifest schema validation exists and is tested.
- Retention/redaction rules are explicit and enforceable.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.023599244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:34:18.442449207Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T13:34:18.442002550Z","close_reason":"Fixed 8 pre-existing clippy lints in ffs-harness: 3 assertions_on_constants → const{assert!()}, 4 float_cmp → #[expect], 1 similar_names → clearer variable names. Clippy clean, 178 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.023599244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.184645771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.335522785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":359,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","author":"SilverForge","text":"Picking up bd-h6nz.9.3 based on bv actionable-blocker triage. Starting with artifact manifest/schema discovery, then I will reserve the touched surfaces and wire the acceptance tests, E2E coverage, and logging-contract evidence into the implementation.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:55:55Z"},{"id":360,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","author":"SilverForge","text":"Implemented the shared artifact manifest surface by exporting , hardening the manifest types/redaction/validation paths, documenting the retention and redaction contract in scripts/e2e/README.md, and extending the log-contract E2E catalog/script with manifest schema and policy checks. Validation evidence: cargo fmt --check ✅; rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness artifact_manifest -- --nocapture ✅ (34 passed); rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-harness --all-targets ✅; scripts/e2e/ffs_log_contract_e2e.sh ✅ (13/13 PASS, including the new artifact-manifest scenarios). Residual caveat: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ffs-harness --all-targets -- -D warnings still fails on pre-existing unrelated harness lints in perf_triage.rs, benchmark_taxonomy.rs, log_contract.rs, and perf_comparison.rs; the new artifact_manifest.rs lint failures are resolved.","created_at":"2026-03-10T06:14:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","title":"Implement verification runner conventions and machine-parseable result format","description":"## Objective
Implement shared runner conventions for deterministic E2E execution and machine-parseable result publication.

## Scope
1. Standard command envelope and environment capture for E2E invocations.
2. Stable pass/fail JSON result schema with scenario-level details.
3. Exit-code and retry semantics for CI/nightly modes.
4. Conformance checks ensuring per-epic scripts adhere to runner contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner conventions are adopted by all verification gates.
- Machine-parseable result format is validated in automated tests.
- Inconsistent script behavior is detected and reported with actionable output.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682751429Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682038293Z","close_reason":"Implemented verification runner conventions: (1) verification_runner.rs in ffs-harness with parse_e2e_output(), build_manifest_from_parsed(), check_script_conformance(), aggregate_verdict(), merge_scenarios() — 21 unit tests. (2) Extended lib.sh with e2e_emit_json_summary() (auto-emits result.json on exit), e2e_retry() for CI mode. (3) Created run_gate.sh runner wrapper with --ci/--retries/--catalog/--conformance flags, gate-level JSON manifest output. (4) E2E script ffs_verification_runner_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios. Clippy clean, 199 harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.645464252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.885423555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.079471552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","title":"Verification Gate: cross-epic verification toolchain contract (unit + e2e + logging audit)","description":"## Objective
Final acceptance gate for the shared verification contract used across all hardening epics.

## Required Validation
1. Unit tests for scenario-ID, log-schema, and artifact-manifest validators.
2. End-to-end dry runs across representative epics proving runner/result-schema compliance.
3. Structured-log audit proving required correlation fields are present and validated.

## Gate Commands
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture
- cross-epic E2E dry-run pack with manifest/result-schema validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared verification contract is executable and enforced.
- All epic verification gates are wired to this contract gate.
- Contract drift is automatically detected before release acceptance.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214964113Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214268861Z","close_reason":"Final acceptance gate for verification contract: (1) Extended verification_runner.rs with validate_scenario_ids(), validate_gate_contract(), structured logging with operation_id/scenario_id/outcome correlation fields — 26 unit tests. (2) Created ffs_verification_gate_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios covering: artifact_manifest tests (36), log_contract tests (17), verification_runner tests (26), scenario catalog validation, cross-epic script conformance, logging field audit, schema version consistency, marker format sync, full harness suite (204 tests). Clippy clean, all 204 ffs-harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.414570202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.570336006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.724217238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.886155847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-h7gk","title":"Validate RAID10 stripe layout in map_logical_to_stripes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:12:51.924122927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:23:59.299988170Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:23:59.299797749Z","close_reason":"Completed: validate RAID0/RAID10 stripe layout","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hks","title":"ffs-block: Plan write-back cache + dirty tracking (phased)","description":"Future-facing task: define how cached writes become durable.

Deliverables:
- DirtyTracker design: which blocks are dirty, ordering constraints, flush scheduling.
- Interaction with MVCC: versioned writes vs overwriting.
- Failure/cancellation semantics (fsync, flush on close, etc).

Acceptance:
- A concrete design exists in docs/spec before any write-back code is added.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:07.481262948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342359407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342335122Z","close_reason":"Design doc created at docs/design-writeback-cache.md with 3-phase plan: DirtyTracker, FlushDaemon, MVCC integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-220","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.218279466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.135355880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hrv","title":"MVCC: Deterministic concurrency tests (asupersync lab runtime)","description":"Goal: make MVCC correctness testable under controlled schedules.

Deliverables:
- Add tests that run transactions under deterministic scheduling (asupersync lab runtime) and explore interleavings.
- Encode invariants: snapshot visibility, FCW conflicts, no lost updates.
- Add at least one regression test for a known anomaly pattern (write skew) once SSI is introduced.

Acceptance:
- Tests are deterministic and non-flaky.
- A small set of schedules is enough to reproduce bugs reliably.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:40.713789731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:33:22.088331821Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:33:22.088246271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hrv","depends_on_id":"bd-19k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.264230369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hrv","depends_on_id":"bd-2t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:23:53.183269997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hu2c","title":"Add proptest property-based tests for ffs-block cache and IO scheduling","notes":"Added 12 new proptest property-based tests covering: DirtyTracker state_counts partition, dirty_bytes accounting, oldest_age_ticks monotonicity, FlushDaemonConfig validate (valid configs, zero interval rejection, inverted watermark rejection), MemoryPressure ordering, ArcState stage/commit/abort semantics, staged block ownership. Total proptest count: 34 (was 22). All 124 tests pass, clippy/fmt clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T10:10:21.003816737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:09:51.070720458Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T11:09:51.070701743Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
